CAT06-015.headline: Growing Up With Addiction
CAT06-015.subtitle: How an addicted parent shapes adult mental health
CAT06-015.figureLine: A small clay figure stands close beside a much larger reclining clay figure, posture watchful and self-contained, conveying a child who learned early to hold steady on their own.
CAT06-015.sceneLine: A single soft light red thread runs from the larger form to the smaller one and continues onward as a pale red line into open space, suggesting an early bond whose pull is carried quietly into adult life.

CAT08-038.headline: Reducing Alcohol Use
CAT08-038.subtitle: Cutting back without full abstinence
CAT08-038.figureLine: A single standing clay figure lowers one open hand in a calm, measured gesture, posture relaxed and intentional, conveying a deliberate choice to ease back rather than abandon all at once.
CAT08-038.sceneLine: A tall red accent column of rounded forms beside the figure steps gently downward into softer pale red shapes, suggesting a measured, dignified reduction rather than a sudden break.

CAT08-039.headline: Shopping Addiction
CAT08-039.subtitle: When buying becomes a coping mechanism
CAT08-039.figureLine: A single seated clay figure cradles a small empty bowl in both hands, posture longing and unsettled, conveying a hunger that buying never quite fills.
CAT08-039.sceneLine: A loose spiral of soft light red rounded forms circles the figure and dissolves into pale red haze, suggesting an appealing loop that keeps turning without ever arriving at rest.

CAT08-057.headline: Understanding Relapse
CAT08-057.subtitle: Why setbacks happen and how to recover
CAT08-057.figureLine: A single kneeling clay figure presses one hand to the ground and begins to rise again, posture effortful but unbroken, conveying a setback met with patient return rather than defeat.
CAT08-057.sceneLine: A red accent path of stepping forms beside the figure dips into a shallow gap and then continues upward in pale red, suggesting that a stumble is part of the road and not its end.

CAT11-044.headline: Self-Medication Hypothesis
CAT11-044.subtitle: How trauma and substance use connect
CAT11-044.figureLine: A single seated clay figure draws a thin sheltering form across its shoulders, posture inward and self-protective, conveying an old hurt being soothed in the only way once known.
CAT11-044.sceneLine: A faint deep red shadow lingers behind the figure while a soft light red shape rests over it like a thin shield, suggesting relief reached for to quiet an older, unhealed ache.

CAT13-029.headline: Trauma And Substance Use
CAT13-029.subtitle: Understanding the connection between them
CAT13-029.figureLine: A single standing clay figure holds two interlinked forms in its hands and turns them slowly to see how they join, posture thoughtful and steadying, conveying the work of understanding how two struggles intertwine.
CAT13-029.sceneLine: Two pale red strands beside the figure twist around one another and then gently part into separate soft light red lines, suggesting two entangled experiences being carefully untangled and understood.

CAT17-008.headline: Addiction In Older Adults
CAT17-008.subtitle: An overlooked crisis later in life
CAT17-008.figureLine: A single seated clay figure rests with quiet dignity, one hand reaching slightly outward as if waiting to be seen, posture composed yet overlooked, conveying a later-life struggle that too often goes unnoticed.
CAT17-008.sceneLine: A soft light red glow surrounds the figure while the deep red space around it stays dim, suggesting a quiet presence deserving of attention that the surrounding world has missed.

CAT19-008.headline: Alcohol As Default Coping
CAT19-008.subtitle: Why men turn to it under pressure
CAT19-008.figureLine: A single standing clay figure holds one hand pressed firmly against its own chest, posture braced and outwardly composed, conveying pressure carried silently behind a steady front.
CAT19-008.sceneLine: A heavy deep red shadow gathers behind the figure while a single soft light red form rests just out of reach, suggesting an easy default leaned on instead of the harder work of being met and heard.

CAT20-019.headline: Opioids And Chronic Pain
CAT20-019.subtitle: Navigating treatment and mental health
CAT20-019.figureLine: A single clay figure walks a careful line with both arms gently extended for balance, posture measured and attentive, conveying the delicate work of managing pain and wellbeing at once.
CAT20-019.sceneLine: A narrow red accent ribbon stretches ahead of the figure between two areas of pale red space, suggesting a careful balanced path threaded between relief and risk.
